# Short-Form Video Best Practices

A knowledge base for creating high-retention YouTube Shorts (≤60s, 1080×1920).

## Hook (0–3 seconds)

- Open with a **question**, surprising stat, or visual action — never a cold intro
- Avoid "Hey guys, welcome back…" — viewer drop-off peaks in the first 2 seconds
- Pattern interrupt: cut mid-sentence, start with a close-up, or use bold text
- **Template:** "You've been doing X wrong — here's why" / "What happens when…"

## Retention Curves

| Timestamp | Goal |
|-----------|------|
| 0–3s | Hook: viewer decides to stay or leave |
| 3–15s | Establish value: "here's what you'll learn/see" |
| 15–45s | Deliver: dense information, no filler |
| 45–58s | CTA or loop-back: "watch again to catch the detail" |

## Captions

- **Always on**: 40% of viewers watch without audio
- Word-by-word karaoke style outperforms static subtitles by ~23% on average watch time
- Max 5–7 words per line; keep caps minimal (only proper nouns)
- High contrast: white text with thick black stroke OR black on yellow
- Position: bottom-third preferred; avoid covering faces

## B-Roll Cadence

- Insert b-roll every **8–12 seconds** over talking-head segments
- Each b-roll clip: 2–3 seconds max
- Match visual to spoken word (not random stock footage)
- Skip b-roll in the first 3 seconds — stay on the speaker for the hook

## Vertical Framing (1080×1920)

- Keep subjects in the **upper two-thirds** of frame (lower-third holds captions)
- For reformatting landscape → vertical: face-track crop > smart crop > letterbox
- Blurred-background letterbox is acceptable but loses ~15% of real estate
- Never stretch or distort — always scale to fit then pad or crop

## Audio

- Target loudness: **-14 LUFS** for music bed, voiceover at -6 LUFS
- Fade music in/out over 0.3s at clip boundaries
- Background music at 20–30% of voiceover volume
- Always include captions for accessibility even if audio is clear

## YouTube Shorts Algorithm Signals

- **Watch-through rate** is the primary ranking signal (aim >70%)
- Loops count: structure your Short so it naturally loops (end connects to start)
- Comment bait: end with a question or controversial take
- Upload cadence: consistency (3–5x/week) matters more than virality
- Use `#Shorts` in description (auto-detected but good practice)
- Optimal duration: **30–45 seconds** often outperforms the full 60s

## Caption Presets Guide

| Preset | Best For | Style |
|--------|----------|-------|
| `bold-pop` | High-energy, entertainment, gaming | White/red, large, pop animation |
| `subtle-bottom` | Educational, explainers | Gray-white, smaller, fade animation |
| `glow-center` | Music, art, aesthetic content | Neon, centered, karaoke |
| `meme` | Comedy, reaction content | Impact-style, top position |

## Common Mistakes

- ❌ Starting with "So today I want to talk about…"
- ❌ Slow zoom-ins that take >0.5s to settle
- ❌ Music louder than voiceover
- ❌ More than 8 words on-screen at once
- ❌ Uploading landscape video without reformatting
- ❌ No CTA or loop mechanic at the end
